title: Flawed Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown:
Given a sorted doubly linked list, merge two sorted doubly linked lists in reverse order.

The merged list should also be sorted in reverse order.

Please ensure that every node in the merged list only points to the next node.

You should not modify the input linked lists.
statement.inputFormat:
Input will consist of a doubly linked list in the format: head -> node1 -> node2 -> ... -> tail

The next node can be obtained by calling next node's head or next node's value.
statement.outputFormat:
Print the merged list in reverse order.

The merged list should also be sorted in reverse order.
statement.notes: You are supposed to create a new linked list and return the head of this new list.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def mergeSort(self, head, tail):
        if head is None or tail is None:
            return None
        dummy = Node(0)
        dummy.next = head
        newTail = tail
        prev = dummy
        while head != tail:
            if head.next == tail:
                break
            if head.val > newTail.val:
                node = Node(head.val)
                prev.next = node
                node.next = head
                prev = node
                head = head.next
                newTail = node
            elif head.next == newTail:
                node = Node(head.val)
                prev.next = node
                node.next = head
                prev = node
                newTail = node
                head = head.next
            else:
                prev = head
                head = head.next
        newtail.next = None
        return dummy.next

    def mergeSortRecur(self, head, tail):
        if head == tail:
            return
        self.mergeSortRecur(head, tail.next)
        while head.next != tail:
            if head.val > head.next.val:
                node = Node(head.val)
                tail.next = node
                node.next = head.next
                node.prev = tail
                head.next.prev = node
                tail = node
            head = head.next
